# RQ-0947 - Phase 2 Execution Directive

## Task Goal
Deliver durable CI escalation behavior for repeated identical failures: when the CI gate fails with the same detected error pattern for `N` consecutive attempts (default `N=3`), Ralph must stop generic compliance retries and escalate with explicit manual-intervention guidance.

## Why
A real run showed five identical CI failures with no forward progress. Re-sending the same compliance message is not enough when the root cause is unchanged. Ralph must detect this no-progress loop and escalate deterministically so humans can intervene.

## Current Reality (verified in code)
- Core escalation plumbing already exists in the tree:
  - `CI_FAILURE_ESCALATION_THRESHOLD` is defined in `crates/ralph/src/constants.rs`.
  - `ContinueSession` already tracks `last_ci_error_pattern` and `consecutive_same_error_count` in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/continue_session.rs`.
  - `run_ci_gate_with_continue_session` already compares patterns and bails on threshold in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/ci.rs`.
- The missing durable guard is behavioral regression coverage of the escalation control path itself (same-pattern threshold, reset behavior, and success reset).

## Strategic Intent
Do not re-implement existing scaffolding unless tests prove behavior is wrong. Prefer minimal production edits and strong tests that lock the escalation contract.

If file layout differs from this directive, adapt to equivalent modules and keep behavior identical.

## Required Changes

### 1) Lock escalation behavior with focused `ci.rs` tests
File: `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/ci.rs`

Add a test helper in the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` to build a valid `ContinueSession` for CI loop tests:

```rust
fn continue_session_for_ci_tests() -> crate::commands::run::supervision::ContinueSession {
    crate::commands::run::supervision::ContinueSession {
        runner: Runner::Codex,
        model: crate::contracts::Model::Gpt52Codex,
        reasoning_effort: None,
        runner_cli: crate::runner::ResolvedRunnerCliOptions::default(),
        phase_type: crate::commands::run::PhaseType::Implementation,
        session_id: Some("sess-123".to_string()),
        output_handler: None,
        output_stream: crate::runner::OutputStream::Terminal,
        ci_failure_retry_count: CI_GATE_AUTO_RETRY_LIMIT,
        task_id: "RQ-0947".to_string(),
        last_ci_error_pattern: None,
        consecutive_same_error_count: 0,
    }
}
```

Add at least these three tests (exact names can vary):

```rust
#[test]
fn run_ci_gate_with_continue_session_escalates_on_threshold_same_pattern() -> Result<()> {
    let temp = TempDir::new()?;
    let command = if cfg!(windows) {
        r#"powershell -NoProfile -Command "Write-Error 'ruff failed: TOML parse error at line 44'; exit 1""#
    } else {
        r#"sh -c 'echo "ruff failed: TOML parse error at line 44" >&2; exit 1'"#
    };

    let resolved = resolved_with_ci_command(temp.path(), Some(command.to_string()), true);
    let mut session = continue_session_for_ci_tests();
    session.ci_failure_retry_count = 0;
    session.last_ci_error_pattern = Some("TOML parse error".to_string());
    session.consecutive_same_error_count = CI_FAILURE_ESCALATION_THRESHOLD - 1;

    let err = run_ci_gate_with_continue_session(
        &resolved,
        crate::contracts::GitRevertMode::Disabled,
        None,
        &mut session,
        |_output, _elapsed| -> Result<()> { panic!("on_resume should not be called") },
        None,
    )
    .expect_err("expected escalation on repeated identical CI error");

    let msg = err.to_string();
    assert!(msg.contains("MANUAL INTERVENTION REQUIRED"));
    assert!(msg.contains("same error"));
    assert!(msg.contains("TOML parse error"));
    assert_eq!(session.consecutive_same_error_count, CI_FAILURE_ESCALATION_THRESHOLD);
    Ok(())
}

#[test]
fn run_ci_gate_with_continue_session_resets_counter_when_pattern_changes() -> Result<()> {
    let temp = TempDir::new()?;
    let command = if cfg!(windows) {
        r#"powershell -NoProfile -Command "Write-Error 'format-check failed'; exit 1""#
    } else {
        r#"sh -c 'echo "format-check failed" >&2; exit 1'"#
    };

    let resolved = resolved_with_ci_command(temp.path(), Some(command.to_string()), true);
    let mut session = continue_session_for_ci_tests();
    session.ci_failure_retry_count = CI_GATE_AUTO_RETRY_LIMIT;
    session.last_ci_error_pattern = Some("TOML parse error".to_string());
    session.consecutive_same_error_count = CI_FAILURE_ESCALATION_THRESHOLD - 1;

    let _ = run_ci_gate_with_continue_session(
        &resolved,
        crate::contracts::GitRevertMode::Disabled,
        None,
        &mut session,
        |_output, _elapsed| -> Result<()> { panic!("on_resume should not be called") },
        None,
    )
    .expect_err("expected CI failure after counter reset path");

    assert_eq!(session.consecutive_same_error_count, 1);
    assert_eq!(
        session.last_ci_error_pattern.as_deref(),
        Some("Format check failure")
    );
    Ok(())
}

#[test]
fn run_ci_gate_with_continue_session_clears_pattern_tracking_after_success() -> Result<()> {
    let temp = TempDir::new()?;
    let command = if cfg!(windows) {
        r#"powershell -NoProfile -Command "exit 0""#
    } else {
        r#"sh -c 'exit 0'"#
    };

    let resolved = resolved_with_ci_command(temp.path(), Some(command.to_string()), true);
    let mut session = continue_session_for_ci_tests();
    session.last_ci_error_pattern = Some("TOML parse error".to_string());
    session.consecutive_same_error_count = 2;

    run_ci_gate_with_continue_session(
        &resolved,
        crate::contracts::GitRevertMode::Disabled,
        None,
        &mut session,
        |_output, _elapsed| -> Result<()> { Ok(()) },
        None,
    )?;

    assert_eq!(session.last_ci_error_pattern, None);
    assert_eq!(session.consecutive_same_error_count, 0);
    Ok(())
}
```

Notes for implementation:
- Keep these tests inside `ci.rs`’s existing test module to avoid exposing internals.
- Avoid resume-session dependency in these tests by setting `ci_failure_retry_count` to limit where appropriate.

### 2) Add one end-to-end escalation regression test
File: `crates/ralph/tests/git_repo_ci_gate_compliance_message_test.rs` (or a new adjacent integration test file)

Add a test that runs `ralph run one` with:
- CI script that always fails with the same detectable pattern (`TOML parse error ...`).
- Fake runner that records resume invocations to a counter file.

Expected assertions:
- Run fails.
- stderr includes escalation language (`MANUAL INTERVENTION REQUIRED` and repeated-same-error wording).
- Resume count stops before generic limit exhaustion (for threshold 3, expect 2 continue attempts before escalation on the 3rd failure).

Skeleton:

```rust
#[test]
fn ci_gate_repeated_same_error_escalates_before_retry_limit() -> Result<()> {
    // mirror setup from existing compliance-message integration tests
    // configure CI script to always emit TOML parse error and exit non-zero
    // configure fake runner to append each resume call into count file
    // run: ralph run one --git-revert-mode disabled
    // assert failure + escalation text + expected resume count
    Ok(())
}
```

### 3) Verify no unintended side effects in failure wrapping
Files to inspect while implementing tests:
- `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/mod.rs`
- `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/parallel_worker.rs`

If new tests reveal duplicate/conflicting revert/failure messaging from nested error handling, make the minimal fix in supervision flow so escalation produces one coherent failure outcome.

## Behavior Contract (must pass)
- Same detected pattern repeated 3 times escalates immediately.
- Escalation error includes manual intervention guidance.
- Pattern change resets consecutive counter to `1`.
- CI pass resets `last_ci_error_pattern=None` and `consecutive_same_error_count=0`.
- Existing generic retry path still works for non-threshold cases.

## Phase 2 Validation Commands
Run these after changes:

```bash
cargo test -p ralph run_ci_gate_with_continue_session_escalates_on_threshold_same_pattern
cargo test -p ralph run_ci_gate_with_continue_session_resets_counter_when_pattern_changes
cargo test -p ralph run_ci_gate_with_continue_session_clears_pattern_tracking_after_success
cargo test -p ralph --test git_repo_ci_gate_compliance_message_test
make macos-ci
```

If `make macos-ci` is unavailable in the environment, run the repo’s configured CI gate command and document the substitution explicitly in the completion summary.
